title: Ghanteswari Temple
tagline: Ghanteswari Temple — spiritual town of Odisha

overview.short: Maa Ghanteshwari temple is a temple located in Chiplima which is 30 km from Sambalpur city via NH 6 in Odisha, India as of now.
overview.description: Maa Ghanteshwari temple is a temple located in Chiplima which is 30 km from Sambalpur city via NH 6 in Odisha, India as of now. A bridge is constructed (called Chaurpur bridge) at Mundoghat a strategically important place between Sambalpur and Chiplima. As of December 2018, it is fully functional. It has halved the distance between Maa Ghanteswari Temple and Sambalpur.

topPlaces.name: Chipilima Hydro Electric Project
topPlaces.description: Chipilima Hydro Electric Project (CHEP) is a hydropower plant located at Chipilima, Sambalpur, Odisha, India. The Odisha government has plans for the modernisation of 5th and 6th unit of Burla Hydro Electric Project (BHEP) and 3rd unit of Chipilima Hydro Electric Project (CHEP) during the 11th five year plan tenure.

topPlaces.name: Leaning Temple of Huma
topPlaces.description: The Leaning Temple of Huma in India is one of only a very few leaning temples in the world. It is located in Huma, a village situated on the bank of the Mahanadi, 23 km (14 mi) south of Sambalpur in the Indian state of Odisha.

topPlaces.name: Kadobahal
topPlaces.description: Kadobahal is a village in Attabira tehsil, Bargarh District, Odisha, India. This village is situated on a side of State Highway No.

topPlaces.name: Chhatabar
topPlaces.description: Chhatabar is a village in the Sambalpur district of Odisha, India. (There is another village called Chhatabar in Jatani block, Khordha district, Odisha).

itinerary.title: Day 1 — Chipilima Hydro Electric Project & around

items.note: Chipilima Hydro Electric Project (CHEP) is a hydropower plant located at Chipilima, Sambalpur, Odisha, India. The Odisha government has plans for the modernisation of 5th and 6th unit of Burla Hydro Electric Project (BHEP) and 3rd unit of Chipilima Hydro Electric Project (CHEP) during the 11th five year plan tenure.

The Leaning Temple of Huma in India is one of only a very few leaning temples in the world. It is located in Huma, a village situated on the bank of the Mahanadi, 23 km (14 mi) south of Sambalpur in the Indian state of Odisha.
Kadobahal is a village in Attabira tehsil, Bargarh District, Odisha, India. This village is situated on a side of State Highway No.
Chhatabar is a village in the Sambalpur district of Odisha, India. (There is another village called Chhatabar in Jatani block, Khordha district, Odisha).

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Ghanteswari Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Ghanteswari Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Ghanteswari Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Swami Vivekananda Airport, Raipur (227 km) and the nearest railway station is Sambalpur Junction (14 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Ghanteswari Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Ghanteswari Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Ghanteswari Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Ghanteswari Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Ghanteswari Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Chipilima Hydro Electric Project, Leaning Temple of Huma, Kadobahal, Chhatabar.
